text_excerpt: In Parliament - Catherine West Skip to main content Catherine West Working hard for Hornsey & Friern Barnet Join Get Involved Donate About In Parliament Latest news FAQs Advice My e-newsletter Monthly Enews Contact More Menu In Parliament Members of Parliament are elected to represent the interests and concerns of the people who live in their constituency. MPs consider and vote on legislation and use their position to ask Government Ministers questions about current issues. Once a month each Minister from each Government Department answers questions from MPs at the Dispatch Box. Below you can find a live feed detailing the most recent updates on Catherine’s Parliamentary activity. You can also view her speeches and interventions in Parliament here and her written parliamentary questions here. Points of Order 29 Apr 2026 Commons debates On a point of order, Mr Speaker. The whole House will be very concerned to hear about the dreadful stabbings this morning in the borough of Barnet. As the Member for Hornsey and Friern Barnet, I...
